content:
## Introduction

The Core Information Standard v2.0 defines the essential data elements for person-centered health and care records across the UK. This standard establishes a comprehensive framework for capturing demographic information, clinical data, social care needs, and personal preferences in a structured, interoperable format.

### Purpose and Scope

This Digital Adaptation Kit (DAK) provides implementation guidance for the Core Information Standard v2.0, supporting healthcare organizations, social care providers, and technology vendors in adopting this foundational standard. The standard covers the complete spectrum of person-centered information from basic demographics through complex care planning, legal frameworks, and end-of-life preferences.

### Background

Developed through extensive stakeholder consultation, the Core Information Standard v2.0 represents a significant evolution in integrated health and social care documentation. It emphasizes person-centered care by incorporating "About Me" sections alongside traditional clinical data, ensuring that individual preferences, communication needs, and personal circumstances are captured systematically. The standard aligns with UK regulatory requirements including the Equality Act 2010, Mental Capacity Act 2005, and supports initiatives like the Accessible Information Standard.
title: Introduction

content:
## Health Interventions & Recommendations

The Core Information Standard v2.0 enables comprehensive documentation of health interventions across multiple care contexts. Key recommendations focus on person-centered implementation and data quality.

### Key Recommendations

| Intervention | Recommendation | Source | Grade |
|---|---|---|---|
| Reasonable Adjustments | Mandatory documentation per Equality Act 2010; use SNOMED CT coding (1108111000000107) | NHS England Accessible Information Standard | A |
| Mental Capacity Assessment | Document capacity decisions with clear rationale; include best interests decisions where applicable | Mental Capacity Act 2005 | A |
| Medication Management | Use dm+d coding for all medications; document administration details and discontinuation reasons | NHS Digital Dose Syntax Guidance | A |
| Care Planning | Integrate person's goals, strengths, and preferences; document agreement with person/representative | NICE Guidelines on Person-Centered Care | B |
| Risk Assessment | Document risk type, mitigation strategies, and review dates; include self-harm and safeguarding risks | NHS Safeguarding Guidelines | A |
| Advance Care Planning | Record ADRT, LPA details, and preferred place of care/death with regular review | Gold Standards Framework | B |
| Communication Support | Document communication methods, aids required, and language preferences | Accessible Information Standard | A |
| Social Care Needs | Capture primary support reasons, care package details, and funding arrangements | Care Act 2014 Guidance | A |

### Context

These recommendations emphasize the importance of capturing both clinical and social determinants of health. The standard's comprehensive approach ensures continuity of care across settings while maintaining focus on individual preferences and legal requirements.
title: Health Interventions & Recommendations

content:
## Generic Personas

The following personas represent key users and stakeholders of the Core Information Standard v2.0 implementation.

| Persona ID | Title | Description | Key Competencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.P1 | GP/Primary Care Physician | Manages ongoing care for patients in community settings; needs comprehensive view of medical history, medications, and social circumstances | Clinical assessment, medication management, care coordination, SNOMED CT coding |
| CISV2.P2 | Social Worker | Coordinates social care services and safeguarding; requires access to legal information, care plans, and support networks | Safeguarding assessment, care planning, Mental Capacity Act knowledge, multi-agency coordination |
| CISV2.P3 | Community Nurse | Provides nursing care in home/community settings; needs medication information, care plans, and communication preferences | Clinical procedures, medication administration, wound care, patient education |
| CISV2.P4 | Hospital Consultant | Specialist providing acute/specialist care; requires clinical history, investigation results, and advance decisions | Specialist clinical knowledge, diagnostic interpretation, treatment planning |
| CISV2.P5 | Care Home Manager | Oversees residential care delivery; needs comprehensive personal information, care plans, and legal documentation | Care quality management, staff supervision, regulatory compliance |
| CISV2.P6 | Patient/Service User | Person receiving care; wants control over personal information sharing and care preferences | Self-advocacy, health literacy (variable), personal preference expression |
| CISV2.P7 | Family Carer | Provides informal care and advocacy; needs understanding of care plans and communication methods | Care provision, advocacy, emotional support, care coordination |
| CISV2.P8 | Paramedic/Emergency Responder | Provides urgent/emergency care; requires rapid access to critical information including allergies and advance decisions | Emergency assessment, rapid decision-making, clinical procedures |

### Persona Context

These personas interact with different aspects of the Core Information Standard based on their roles. GPs and consultants focus on clinical sections, while social workers emphasize legal frameworks and support needs. All personas benefit from the person-centered "About Me" information for delivering appropriate care.
title: Generic Personas

content:
## User Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Emergency Hospital Admission

**Narrative**: Mrs. Chen, 78, is brought to A&E following a fall at home. She has moderate dementia and limited English proficiency. The paramedics access her Core Information record via the Summary Care Record.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Paramedic (CISV2.P8), A&E Consultant (CISV2.P4), Mrs. Chen (CISV2.P6), Daughter (CISV2.P7) |
| Trigger | Emergency call reporting fall with confusion |
| Key Data Used | Reasonable adjustments (interpreter needed), medications list, DNACPR status, emergency contacts, cognitive impairment details |
| Outcome | Appropriate care provided with interpreter support; daughter contacted; admission avoided through community support |
| Linked Processes | Emergency assessment, medication reconciliation, discharge planning |

### Scenario 2: Social Care Review

**Narrative**: Mr. Kumar's annual care package review identifies increasing mobility difficulties. His social worker coordinates with health professionals to reassess needs.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | Social Worker (CISV2.P2), Mr. Kumar (CISV2.P6), Community OT, Care Provider |
| Trigger | Scheduled annual review plus reported mobility changes |
| Key Data Used | Care and support plan, assessment history, goals and outcomes, equipment provided, primary support reason |
| Outcome | Care package adjusted with additional mobility aids; referral to falls prevention service |
| Linked Processes | Needs assessment, care planning, equipment provision, multi-disciplinary review |

### Scenario 3: Medication Change in Primary Care

**Narrative**: Dr. Patel reviews Mr. Thompson's medications following a hospital discharge letter indicating adverse reaction to ACE inhibitor.

| Category | Description |
|---|---|
| Actors | GP (CISV2.P1), Mr. Thompson (CISV2.P6), Practice Pharmacist |
| Trigger | Hospital discharge notification with medication change |
| Key Data Used | Allergies and adverse reactions, current medications, problem list, recent procedures |
| Outcome | Medication safely switched with appropriate coding; allergy record updated; patient counselled |
| Linked Processes | Medication review, allergy documentation, patient education, coding update |
title: User Scenarios

content:
## References

### Standards and Specifications

1. **NHS Data Dictionary** - NHS Digital (2023). Comprehensive data definitions for NHS information standards. Available at: https://datadictionary.nhs.uk/

2. **SNOMED CT UK Edition** - NHS Digital (2023). Clinical terminology for UK healthcare. Available at: https://digital.nhs.uk/services/terminology-and-classifications/snomed-ct

3. **Dictionary of Medicines and Devices (dm+d)** - NHS Digital (2023). UK medicines and devices terminology. Available at: https://www.nhsbsa.nhs.uk/pharmacies-gp-practices-and-appliance-contractors/dictionary-medicines-and-devices-dmd

4. **UK Core FHIR Implementation Guide** - NHS Digital (2023). FHIR profiles for UK use. Available at: https://simplifier.net/ukcore

### Regulatory and Policy Documents

5. **Equality Act 2010** - UK Government. Legislative framework for reasonable adjustments. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2010/15/contents

6. **Mental Capacity Act 2005** - UK Government. Framework for capacity assessment and best interests decisions. Available at: https://www.legislation.gov.uk/ukpga/2005/9/contents

7. **Accessible Information Standard** - NHS England (2016). Requirements for accessible information and communication support. Available at: https://www.england.nhs.uk/ourwork/accessibleinfo/

8. **Care Act 2014 Statutory Guidance** - Department of Health and Social Care (2023). Guidance on adult social care duties. Available at: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/care-act-statutory-guidance/care-and-support-statutory-guidance

### Clinical Guidelines

9. **NICE Guideline [NG86]: People's Experience in Adult Social Care Services** - NICE (2018). Person-centered care principles.

10. **Gold Standards Framework** - National Gold Standards Framework Centre (2023). End of life care guidance. Available at: https://www.goldstandardsframework.org.uk/

### Technical Implementation

11. **FHIR Dose Syntax Implementation Guidance** - NHS Digital (2023). Structured dose direction implementation.

12. **PRSB Standards for the Structure and Content of Health and Care Records** - Professional Record Standards Body (2023). Available at: https://theprsb.org/standards/

### Related Standards

13. **Summary Care Record Application (SCRa)** - NHS Digital (2023). National summary record system.

14. **Safeguarding Adults: Roles and Competencies for Health Care Staff** - Royal College of Nursing (2018). Intercollegiate guidance document.

15. **Deprivation of Liberty Safeguards Code of Practice** - Department of Health (2008). DoLS implementation guidance.
title: References

content: The Core Information Standard v2.0 defines five primary business processes that span initial registration through ongoing care management. Each process involves multiple personas working collaboratively to capture, maintain, and utilize person-centered information effectively.

activities.objectives: Establish person's identity, capture core demographics, and register individual requirements including reasonable adjustments
activities.personas: GP/Primary Care Physician, Care Home Manager, Social Worker
activities.title: Initial Person Registration & Demographics Capture

activities.objectives: Conduct comprehensive health assessment, identify care needs, and develop person-centered care plan
activities.personas: Hospital Consultant, Community Nurse, GP/Primary Care Physician, Patient/Service User, Family Carer
activities.title: Clinical Assessment & Care Planning

activities.objectives: Rapid access to critical information during emergency situations to ensure appropriate and safe care delivery
activities.personas: Paramedic/Emergency Responder, Hospital Consultant, Family Carer
activities.title: Emergency Response & Information Access

activities.objectives: Assess social care needs, determine support requirements, and coordinate care package delivery
activities.personas: Social Worker, Care Home Manager, Community Nurse, Patient/Service User, Family Carer
activities.title: Social Care Needs Assessment

activities.objectives: Establish person's end of life preferences, document advance decisions, and coordinate palliative care
activities.personas: GP/Primary Care Physician, Community Nurse, Hospital Consultant, Patient/Service User, Family Carer
activities.title: End of Life Care Planning

processes_data.summary_markdown: The Core Information Standard v2.0 defines five primary business processes that span initial registration through ongoing care management. Each process involves multiple personas working collaboratively to capture, maintain, and utilize person-centered information effectively.

title: Business Processes & Workflows

content:
## Core Data Elements

The Core Information Standard v2.0 defines essential data elements organized by domain to ensure comprehensive person-centered documentation.

### Person Demographics Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE1 | Person first name | The first name(s) of the person including middle names | String (max 35) | M | NHS_DD: Person given name |
| CISV2.DE2 | Person family name | The family name or surname of the person | String (max 35) | M | NHS_DD: Person family name |
| CISV2.DE3 | Person preferred name | Name by which person wishes to be addressed | String (max 70) | R | NHS_DD: Person full name |
| CISV2.DE4 | Date of birth | The date of birth of the person | Date (YMD) | M | NHS_DD: Person birth date |
| CISV2.DE5 | NHS number | Unique identifier within NHS England/Wales | String (10) | R | NHS_DD: NHS number |
| CISV2.DE6 | Gender | Person's stated gender | String (1) | R | NHS_DD: Person stated gender code |
| CISV2.DE7 | Address line 1 | First line of person's address | String (max 35) | M | NHS_DD: Patient usual address |
| CISV2.DE8 | Postcode | Person's postcode | String (max 8) | R | NHS_DD: Postcode of usual address |

### Individual Requirements Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE9 | Reasonable adjustment flag | Indicates reasonable adjustments required | Boolean | R | SNOMED CT: 1108111000000107 |
| CISV2.DE10 | Reasonable adjustment type | Type of adjustment required | Coded | R | SNOMED CT: Accessible information refsets |
| CISV2.DE11 | Communication method | How person communicates | Text | R | Free text |
| CISV2.DE12 | Impairment category | Category of impairment | Coded | R | SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE13 | Mobility needs | Person's mobility requirements | Coded | R | SNOMED CT: 999002551000000108 |

### Clinical Information Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE14 | Problem | Condition requiring treatment | Coded | M | SNOMED CT: 1127581000000103 |
| CISV2.DE15 | Medication name | Generic or brand name | Coded | M | dm+d: VTM/VMP/AMP |
| CISV2.DE16 | Allergy causative agent | Substance causing allergy | Coded | M | dm+d/SNOMED CT |
| CISV2.DE17 | Investigation performed | Type of investigation | Coded | M | SNOMED CT: 71388002 |
| CISV2.DE18 | Procedure performed | Therapeutic/diagnostic procedure | Coded | M | SNOMED CT: 71388002 |

### Care Planning Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE19 | Care plan goal | Person's goals and hopes | Text/Coded | R | SNOMED CT context |
| CISV2.DE20 | Goal importance score | Importance to person (1-10) | Decimal | R | Numeric scale |
| CISV2.DE21 | Actions planned | Actions to achieve goals | Text/Coded | R | SNOMED CT procedures |
| CISV2.DE22 | Agreed with person | Plan agreement status | Boolean | R | Yes/No |
| CISV2.DE23 | Review date | When plan will be reviewed | DateTime | R | Date/time |

### Legal & Safeguarding Domain

| Element ID | Label | Description | Data Type | Required | Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.DE24 | Mental capacity assessment | Capacity for specific decision | Text | R | Free text |
| CISV2.DE25 | LPA status | Lasting power of attorney | Coded | R | SNOMED CT: 999001951000000107 |
| CISV2.DE26 | DNACPR status | CPR decision status | Coded | R | SNOMED CT: 450475007/450476008 |
| CISV2.DE27 | Safeguarding indicator | Safeguarding concerns flag | Boolean | R | Yes/No |
| CISV2.DE28 | DoLS status | Deprivation of Liberty status | String | R | Requested/Authorised |
title: Core Data Elements

content:
## Decision-Support Logic

The Core Information Standard v2.0 incorporates decision support to guide appropriate documentation and care delivery based on person characteristics and circumstances.

### Decision Table: Reasonable Adjustment Requirements

**Title**: Determining Required Reasonable Adjustments
**Description**: Based on identified impairments and communication needs, determine which reasonable adjustments must be provided under the Equality Act 2010.

| Condition (Input) | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Impairment = Sensory (sight) | Provide information in alternative formats (large print, audio, Braille) |
| Impairment = Sensory (hearing) | Provide BSL interpreter or written communication |
| Impairment = Learning disability | Provide easy-read materials; allow extra time; support person present |
| Impairment = Dementia | Simplify communication; involve carer; provide visual cues |
| Impairment = Physical disability | Ensure accessible facilities; adjust appointment times; home visits |
| Communication need = Non-English speaker | Provide professional interpreter (not family member) |
| Multiple impairments present | Combine adjustments; prioritize based on severity |

### Decision Table: Mental Capacity Assessment Triggers

**Title**: When to Conduct Mental Capacity Assessment
**Description**: Triggers that indicate a mental capacity assessment should be conducted for specific decisions.

| Condition (Input) | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Major treatment decision required | Assess capacity for specific treatment decision |
| Person has dementia diagnosis | Assess capacity for each significant decision |
| Person has learning disability | Consider capacity; adjust communication method |
| Refusal of recommended treatment | Assess capacity to refuse treatment |
| Significant change in mental state | Reassess capacity for ongoing decisions |
| Financial or legal decision needed | Assess capacity for specific decision type |
| Conflicting views (person vs family) | Formal capacity assessment required |

### Decision Table: Safeguarding Response Requirements

**Title**: Safeguarding Response Based on Risk Type
**Description**: Determines required actions based on identified safeguarding concerns.

| Condition (Input) | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Risk of self-harm identified | Immediate risk assessment; mental health referral; safety plan |
| Financial abuse suspected | Adult safeguarding referral; secure finances; document evidence |
| Physical abuse indicators | Safeguarding referral; medical examination; photograph injuries |
| Neglect concerns | Multi-agency meeting; care package review; monitoring plan |
| Child at risk in household | Children's services notification; joint assessment |
| Domestic violence disclosed | Safety planning; specialist service referral; consider MARAC |

### Decision Table: Emergency Information Priority

**Title**: Critical Information for Emergency Response
**Description**: Prioritizes information display for emergency responders based on clinical scenario.

| Condition (Input) | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Cardiac arrest scenario | Display: DNACPR status → Advance decisions → Emergency contacts |
| Anaphylaxis risk | Display: Allergies → Current medications → Emergency contacts |
| Mental health crisis | Display: Mental health act status → Risks → Crisis plan → Contacts |
| Fall with injury | Display: Anticoagulation status → Mobility aids → Emergency contacts |
| Unknown emergency | Display: DNACPR → Allergies → Medications → Key conditions |

### Decision Table: Care Setting Transitions

**Title**: Information Requirements for Care Transitions
**Description**: Determines essential information to transfer based on transition type.

| Condition (Input) | Output Action |
|---|---|
| Hospital to home | Medications changes, follow-up appointments, equipment needs, care plan |
| Home to care home | Full assessment, About Me, legal status, medication list, care preferences |
| Hospital to hospice | EoL preferences, DNACPR, symptom management, family contacts |
| GP to specialist | Reason for referral, current problems, medications, relevant history |
| Emergency admission | Allergies, medications, DNACPR, capacity status, key contacts |
title: Decision-Support Logic

content:
## Indicators & Performance Metrics

Key performance indicators for monitoring implementation and quality of the Core Information Standard v2.0.

### Quality & Completeness Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.KPI01 | Demographic completeness | Records with all mandatory demographic fields complete | Total active records | By organization, care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI02 | Reasonable adjustment documentation | Persons with documented reasonable adjustments | Persons with identified impairments | By impairment type, organization | Quarterly |
| CISV2.KPI03 | About Me completion rate | Records with completed About Me section | Total registered persons | By age group, care setting | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI04 | Care plan currency | Care plans reviewed within specified timeframe | Total care plans requiring review | By service type, risk level | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI05 | DNACPR documentation | Persons with documented CPR decision | Persons identified for EoL care | By setting, diagnosis group | Monthly |

### Safety & Risk Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.KPI06 | Allergy recording | Persons with allergy status recorded | Total registered persons | By allergy type, severity | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI07 | Safeguarding identification | New safeguarding concerns identified | Total assessments conducted | By risk type, age group | Weekly |
| CISV2.KPI08 | Mental capacity documentation | Decisions with capacity assessment documented | Significant decisions made | By decision type, outcome | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI09 | Risk assessment completion | Persons with current risk assessment | Persons requiring risk assessment | By risk category, setting | Monthly |

### Process & Efficiency Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.KPI10 | Emergency access time | Records accessed within 2 minutes | Total emergency access attempts | By access point, time of day | Real-time |
| CISV2.KPI11 | Assessment timeliness | Assessments completed within target | Total assessments required | By assessment type, priority | Weekly |
| CISV2.KPI12 | Information sharing compliance | Appropriate sharing with documented consent | Total information sharing events | By recipient type, purpose | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI13 | Multi-agency coordination | Care plans with multi-agency input | Complex care plans | By lead agency, condition | Quarterly |

### Outcome Indicators

| Indicator ID | Title | Numerator | Denominator | Disaggregation | Frequency |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.KPI14 | Person-centered goal achievement | Goals achieved or partially achieved | Total goals set in care plans | By goal type, timeframe | Quarterly |
| CISV2.KPI15 | Preferred place of death achievement | Deaths in preferred place | Deaths with documented preference | By place type, service | Annual |
| CISV2.KPI16 | Unplanned admissions | Emergency admissions for persons with care plans | Persons with active care plans | By primary condition, age | Monthly |
| CISV2.KPI17 | Communication effectiveness | Successful communications per documented method | Communication attempts | By adjustment type | Quarterly |
title: Indicators & Performance Metrics

content:
## Functional & Non-Functional Requirements

System requirements for implementing the Core Information Standard v2.0 to ensure comprehensive person-centered care documentation.

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.FR01 | Person identification | System must support NHS number as primary identifier with ability to manage temporary numbers and merge duplicate records | High |
| CISV2.FR02 | Reasonable adjustment recording | Capture, display and share reasonable adjustment requirements prominently across all care interfaces per Equality Act 2010 | High |
| CISV2.FR03 | About Me documentation | Enable person/carer to directly input preferences, routines, and communication needs with multimedia support | High |
| CISV2.FR04 | Consent management | Record and track consent decisions for information sharing with audit trail and ability to withdraw/modify | High |
| CISV2.FR05 | Care plan creation | Support collaborative care planning with goals, actions, responsibilities and progress tracking | High |
| CISV2.FR06 | Emergency information access | Provide rapid access to critical information (allergies, DNACPR, medications) within 2 minutes | High |
| CISV2.FR07 | Multi-agency coordination | Enable secure information sharing across health and social care boundaries with role-based access | High |
| CISV2.FR08 | Legal status tracking | Maintain current legal status (MCA, MHA, DoLS) with expiry alerts and review requirements | Medium |
| CISV2.FR09 | Risk assessment tools | Provide structured risk assessment with automated flagging of high-risk scenarios | Medium |
| CISV2.FR10 | Document management | Store and retrieve associated documents (advance decisions, LPA) with version control | Medium |
| CISV2.FR11 | Safeguarding workflow | Support safeguarding referral process with secure information sharing to relevant authorities | High |
| CISV2.FR12 | Medication reconciliation | Compare and reconcile medications across care transitions with interaction checking | High |
| CISV2.FR13 | Assessment scheduling | Schedule and track required assessments with automated reminders for reviews | Medium |
| CISV2.FR14 | Family/carer access | Provide appropriate access for authorized family/carers to view and contribute information | Medium |
| CISV2.FR15 | Audit reporting | Generate compliance and quality reports against standard indicators | Low |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Title | Description | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| CISV2.NFR01 | Availability | System available 99.9% with planned maintenance windows outside core hours | High |
| CISV2.NFR02 | Response time | Page load <3 seconds; search results <5 seconds; emergency access <2 minutes | High |
| CISV2.NFR03 | Security | NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit compliance; role-based access control; encryption at rest and in transit | High |
| CISV2.NFR04 | Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance; screen reader compatible; keyboard navigation; adjustable fonts/contrast | High |
| CISV2.NFR05 | Interoperability | HL7 FHIR R4 UK Core compliance; integrate with PDS, SCR, GP systems | High |
| CISV2.NFR06 | Scalability | Support 100,000 concurrent users; 10 million patient records; linear scaling | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR07 | Mobile compatibility | Responsive design for tablets/smartphones; offline capability for community staff | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR08 | Data retention | Maintain records per NHS retention schedules; support legal holds | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR09 | Disaster recovery | RPO <1 hour; RTO <4 hours; geographic redundancy | High |
| CISV2.NFR10 | Usability | Intuitive interface requiring <2 hours training; context-sensitive help | Medium |
| CISV2.NFR11 | Multi-language support | Interface in Welsh and community languages; translation services integration | Low |
| CISV2.NFR12 | Performance degradation | Graceful degradation under load; priority queuing for emergency access | Medium |
title: Functional & Non-Functional Requirements
